none: Ocean dumping criteria, Proposed revisions : environmental impact statement.

The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) proposed revisions to the Ocean Dumping Criteria to better regulate the disposal of waste in ocean waters. The revisions aim to prevent unreasonable degradation of the marine environment by improving testing and monitoring procedures. This action is part of the Marine Protection, Research, and Sanctuaries Act of 1972, which mandates strict control over ocean dumping to protect human health and marine ecosystems. The changes are intended to ensure that ocean dumping is only permitted when it does not harm the environment, while also considering alternative disposal methods.
Ocean dumping criteria, Proposed revisions : environmental impact statement.

The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) proposed revisions to the Ocean Dumping Criteria to better regulate the disposal of waste in ocean waters. The revisions aim to prevent unreasonable degradation of the marine environment by improving testing and monitoring procedures. This action is part of the Marine Protection, Research, and Sanctuaries Act of 1972, which mandates strict control over ocean dumping to protect human health and marine ecosystems. The changes are intended to ensure that ocean dumping is only permitted when it does not harm the environment, while also considering alternative disposal methods.
Oil and Special Materials Control Division

The proposed revisions to the Ocean Dumping Criteria were initiated by the EPA to enhance the regulatory framework governing waste disposal in ocean waters. This move was driven by growing concerns about the environmental impacts of ocean dumping and the need for better monitoring and testing procedures. While there was some public interest in the implications of these changes, the revisions were largely seen as a necessary step to protect marine ecosystems and human health. Ultimately, the revisions aimed to ensure that any ocean dumping would not lead to significant environmental degradation.

"Ocean dumping should be allowed as an alternative method of disposal though strictly controlled and only when it will not unreasonably degrade or endanger human health or the marine environment." — House Merchant Marine and Fisheries Committee

"The clearly stated intent of the Congress is to permit ocean dumping as an acceptable alternative means of waste disposal under strict regulation as long as there is not 'unreasonable degradation.'" — Congressional reports on MPRSA
